name: Ketogenic Diet
resultsSummary: 66 patients randomized for 3 months. Non-significant kidney volume reduction trend (-0.55% vs +0.79% control). Significant liver volume reduction (-4.73%). eGFR improved +5.5% (creatinine) / +13.9% (cystatin-C). 95% found feasible.

name: KetoCitra (BHB + Citrate)
resultsSummary: 103 patients in 3-month program. eGFR increased 6.3% (P<0.001), BMI improved, pain reduced. Limitation: no control group, company-authored study.

name: High Water Intake
resultsSummary: 184 patients over 3 years. NEGATIVE result: no significant benefit (6.8% vs 7.8% annual kidney growth, P=0.18). Only 52% achieved target urine osmolality.

name: Time-Restricted Eating
resultsSummary: 29 patients, 8-hour eating window vs healthy eating for 12 months. Adherence target not met (60% vs 75% goal). Both groups lost modest weight. Conclusion: weight loss may matter more than eating timing.

name: Metformin
resultsSummary: Phase 2 (TAME-PKD) showed safety and encouraging but non-significant eGFR preservation. Phase 3 now recruiting 1,174 patients — largest PKD trial to date.

name: Mediterranean Diet
resultsSummary: Higher adherence associated with lower oxidative stress and reduced endothelial dysfunction in ADPKD patients. No interventional trial yet.
